Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

I need an introductory book on Ayurveda

Health · asked by user [] · 2007-07-31 · 2 answers
for beginners, to recommend for friends and peers
user [38] · 2007-08-01
Brief intro and bibliography by Dr. Vasant Lad:
http://www.heall.com/body/altmed/treatment/ayurveda/ayurveda.html

Info by Dr. Liladhar Gupta:
http://www.secretsof.com/content/1313

Books by Dr. Partap Chauhan:
http://www.ayurvedic.org/store/list.asp?key=book&c1=books&nm1=Books%20and%20DVDs&nm2=Books

Advanced level:
Sarirasthana, (11 MB) a canto of Agnivesas Caraka Samhita. This is a chapter of a classical treatise on ayurveda dealing with how the body is created, propagating good children, pregnancy, embryology, etc. Useful for all practitioners of Vedic culture especially grihastas.
http://shyamasundaradasa.com/jyotish/resources/downloads.html
user [13] · 2007-08-01
This is the canonical work for introducing Westerners to Auyurveda:

Prakriti: Your Ayurvedic Constitution, by Robert Svoboda

http://www.amazon.com/Prakriti-Ayurvedic-Constitution-Robert-Svoboda/dp/0965620832/
